one_line: Seventh-century Bhinmal mathematician-astronomer who forged the rules of zero at the close of India’s golden age of mathematics.

appearance.age_look: Appears around 35–40, lean medium build, slightly stooped from years of study.
appearance.face: Oval face with a high, broad forehead, medium brown north-Indian complexion, straight nose, sharp but kind eyes with gently arched brows, trimmed moustache without beard, a small vertical sandalwood tilak, default expression calm and focused with a hint of intense curiosity.
appearance.hair: Straight, dark brown-black hair parted in the middle, pulled back into a neat low knot at the nape, just visible under a plain off-white cotton uttariya draped over the head like a light hood; no jewellery on the head.
appearance.costume: A historically grounded late-Gupta/early post-Gupta scholar’s outfit: a knee-length, unstitched off-white cotton dhoti wrapped tightly and pleated in front, bordered with a narrow deep-red stripe; bare upper torso mostly covered by a thin, cream-coloured cotton uttariya (shawl) with subtle pale-ochre checks, draped over the left shoulder and around the body; simple brown leather thong sandals; a narrow dark-indigo cotton waistband; a single small copper armlet on the right upper arm and a plain wooden bead bracelet on the left wrist; no extravagant jewellery, emphasizing a learned householder-scholar from Bhinmal in northwestern India.
appearance.signature: A rectangular birch-bark manuscript bundle tied with a dark-brown cord, its edges darkened with age, one folio half-unrolled in his left hand showing faded dark ink numerals and a distinctive small dot used as a placeholder, while his right hand holds a short reed stylus dipped in a tiny black-ink pot hanging from his waistband.

ref_desc: Brahmagupta is a lean, medium-brown man of about 35 with an oval high-foreheaded face, neat moustache, dark hair tied in a low knot under a cream uttariya hood, wearing an off-white dhoti with deep-red border and subtle-checked shawl, carrying a birch-bark manuscript bundle with dot numerals and a reed stylus, in off-white, deep red, indigo, pale ochre and dark brown tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Brahmagupta: a lean, medium-brown north-Indian man around 35 with an oval face, high forehead, calm sharp eyes, straight nose, neatly trimmed moustache and a small vertical sandalwood tilak, his dark brown-black hair parted and tied in a low knot just visible under a cream-coloured cotton uttariya draped lightly over his head and left shoulder like a hood; he wears a late-Gupta/early post-Gupta scholar’s outfit with an off-white cotton dhoti wrapped to the knees and pleated in front with a narrow deep-red border, a thin cream uttariya with pale-ochre checks covering his bare upper torso and looping around his body, a narrow dark-indigo cotton waistband, simple brown leather thong sandals, a single small copper armlet on his right upper arm and a plain wooden bead bracelet on his left wrist; his signature prop is a rectangular birch-bark manuscript bundle tied with a dark-brown cord, one folio half-unrolled in his left hand showing faded dark ink numerals with a small dot placeholder, while his right hand holds a short reed stylus with a tiny black-ink pot hanging from his waistband; use a restrained palette of off-white, deep red, indigo, pale ochre and dark brown, and present him from multiple angles and poses to clearly show face, hair, clothing details and the manuscript with dot numerals.
